one_step_future_grid: {query_instruction} Starting from the grid marked START, which option shows the grid after one update?
{query_instruction} Starting from the grid marked START, choose the option that matches the next grid.
{query_instruction} Starting from the grid marked START, after one update, which option is correct?
{query_instruction} Starting from the grid marked START, select the option showing the one-step future grid.
{query_instruction} Starting from the grid marked START, which labeled option gives the grid after the next update?
two_step_future_grid: {query_instruction} Starting from the grid marked START, which option shows the grid after two updates?
{query_instruction} Starting from the grid marked START, choose the option that matches the grid after two updates.
{query_instruction} Starting from the grid marked START, after two updates, which option is correct?
{query_instruction} Starting from the grid marked START, select the option showing the two-step future grid.
{query_instruction} Starting from the grid marked START, which labeled option gives the grid after two updates?
one_step_alive_cell_count: {query_instruction} Starting from the grid marked START, after one update, how many cells will be alive?
{query_instruction} Starting from the grid marked START, count the cells that will be alive after one update.
{query_instruction} After one update from the START grid, how many cells are alive?
{query_instruction} Apply one update to the grid marked START. How many cells become or remain alive?
{query_instruction} Using the grid marked START as the source grid, how many cells are alive in the next grid?
one_step_dead_cell_count: {query_instruction} Starting from the grid marked START, after one update, how many cells will be empty?
{query_instruction} Starting from the grid marked START, count the cells that will be empty after one update.
{query_instruction} After one update from the START grid, how many cells are empty?
{query_instruction} Apply one update to the grid marked START. How many cells become or remain empty?
{query_instruction} Using the grid marked START as the source grid, how many cells are empty in the next grid?

code_prompt_defaults.object_description_clean_grid: a cellular-automaton source grid marked START where dark cells are alive and light cells are empty, with labeled future-grid options below
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_lab_panel: a lab-style cellular-automaton source grid marked START where dark cells are alive and light cells are empty, with labeled future-grid options below
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_notebook_grid: a notebook-style cellular-automaton source grid marked START where dark cells are alive and light cells are empty, with labeled future-grid options below
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_one_step_cell_state_c: a cellular-automaton source grid marked START where dark cells are alive and light cells are empty
a lab-style cellular-automaton source grid marked START where dark cells are alive and light cells are empty
a notebook-style cellular-automaton source grid marked START where dark cells are alive and light cells are empty
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_life_future_grid_label: set "annotation" to an object/dictionary with keys "source_grid" and "selected_option", each mapped to that pixel bounding box [x0, y0, x1, y1]
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_life_future_grid_label: set "answer" to the single selected option letter
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_one_step_cell_state_coun: set "annotation" to an array of pixel-space bounding boxes [x0, y0, x1, y1], one for each visible source-grid cell that has the requested state after one update; use an empty array if there are none
